title: Measurement, SI Units, Dimensional Analysis, and Uncertainty · Assumption Clinic
summary: Measure a room, select materials, convert specifications, and report tolerances for a community repair. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a unit-safe materials estimate and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose appropriate SI base and derived units and write their symbols correctly.
Convert quantities with unit factors that equal one and cancel dimensions explicitly.
Square or cube conversion factors when converting area and volume.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Order the regression proof

steps.title: Recalculate the repaired case
steps.instruction: A rectangular working area measures 5 m by 3 m. Calculate its area. Use the result to decide Unit Validation for a unit-safe materials estimate.
steps.why: The Unit Validation result for a unit-safe materials estimate is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Multiply perpendicular lengths and square the length unit: 5 m × 3 m. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use dimensional consistency to detect formula and data-entry errors before calculation.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Before calculating Unit Validation, predict the sign and approximate magnitude for a unit-safe materials estimate. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then reconcile it with that prediction and A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use dimensional consistency to detect formula and data-entry errors before calculation.

hints: Multiply perpendicular lengths and square the length unit: 5 m × 3 m.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes m² or m^2 or square meters.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Unit Validation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Use a changed debug case for math beginner measurement units that tests this competency: Use dimensional consistency to detect formula and data-entry errors before calculation.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
steps.why: Retrieving Unit Validation through the concrete a unit-safe materials estimate case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use dimensional consistency to detect formula and data-entry errors before calculation.
